description: Universal AIDA Video Block Taxonomy. Block codes are product-agnostic anchors. Categories and subtypes are defined per brand.

A1.name: ProductShowcase
A1.description: Hero product beauty shot or slideshow

A3.name: PositiveReactions
A3.description: Compilation of approving, surprised, or excited reactions

A4.name: SocialProofGrid
A4.description: Multiple faces or testimonial grid for social proof

A5.name: PainScenario
A5.description: Relatable problem situation the audience identifies with

interest.description: Build trust — show why this product is worth attention

I2.name: ProcessMethod
I2.description: How the product is made, applied, or used

I3.name: PremiumMaterials
I3.description: Quality ingredients, materials, or components closeup

I5.name: RawReviews
I5.description: Unedited review screenshots, messages, or clips

desire.description: Create want — show the experience and outcome

D1.name: UsageScene
D1.description: Person using, enjoying, or experiencing the product

D2.name: ReceiveScene
D2.description: Delivery received, package at door, handover moment

D5.name: LifestyleShot
D5.description: Product in aspirational, aesthetic, or lifestyle context

D6.name: ComparisonDemo
D6.description: Versus competitor, old way, or alternative product

action.description: Close — drive the viewer to act

Act1.name: PackagingShot
Act1.description: Product packaging beauty shot or switch reveal

Act2.name: BundleGroup
Act2.description: Bundle, gift set, or multi-item pop-out

Act3.name: PromoVisual
Act3.description: Promotional image, offer card, or combo deal visual

Act6.name: EndCard
Act6.description: CTA freeze, closing card with logo, PM link, or QR

phase_order_note: Sequences must follow strict A-I-D-Act ordering. Repeats within a phase are allowed but phase order is never violated.
